scores.correctness_notes: Correctly identifies provides/requires/peers structure, all four fields (interface, limit, optional, scope) with accurate descriptions and types. Minor note: the gold standard specifies interface name restrictions (cannot be 'juju' or start with 'juju-', only a-z and -) which the agent omits, but core content is accurate.
scores.hallucination_notes: The last bullet references 'Per the project's CLAUDE.md, always include the optional key on every relation' — this is a fabricated rule. The gold standard says optional 'should always be explicitly included other than for peers', but this comes from charmcraft docs, not CLAUDE.md. The agent misattributes it to CLAUDE.md. Also the description of optional as 'informational only' is slightly misleading — it has functional meaning for whether the charm can be deployed without the relation.
